configurations.title: Get code security configurations for an enterprise

parameters.name: enterprise
parameters.description: The slug version of the enterprise name.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: A cursor, as given in the Link header. If specified, the query only searches for results before this cursor. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: A cursor, as given in the Link header. If specified, the query only searches for results after this cursor. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

configurations.descriptionHTML:
Lists all code security configurations available in an enterprise.

The authenticated user must be an administrator of the enterprise in order to use this endpoint.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the read:enterprise scope to use this endpoint.

Note

The enablement status will only be returned for installed security products.

example.name: High risk settings
example.description: This is a code security configuration for octo-enterprise high risk repositories

example.name: Low risk settings
example.description: This is a code security configuration for octo-enterprise low risk repositories

id.description: The ID of the code security configuration

name.description: The name of the code security configuration. Must be unique within the organization.

target_type.description: The type of the code security configuration.

description.description: A description of the code security configuration

advanced_security.description: The enablement status of GitHub Advanced Security

dependency_graph.description: The enablement status of Dependency Graph

dependabot_alerts.description: The enablement status of Dependabot alerts

dependabot_security_updates.description: The enablement status of Dependabot security updates

dependabot_delegated_alert_dismissal.description: The enablement status of Dependabot delegated alert dismissal

code_scanning_default_setup.description: The enablement status of code scanning default setup

code_scanning_default_setup_options.description: Feature options for code scanning default setup

runner_type.description: Whether to use labeled runners or standard GitHub runners.

runner_label.description: The label of the runner to use for code scanning when runner_type is 'labeled'.

code_scanning_delegated_alert_dismissal.description: The enablement status of code scanning delegated alert dismissal

secret_scanning.description: The enablement status of secret scanning

secret_scanning_push_protection.description: The enablement status of secret scanning push protection

security_configuration_id.description: The ID of the security configuration associated with this bypass reviewer

secret_scanning_validity_checks.description: The enablement status of secret scanning validity checks

secret_scanning_non_provider_patterns.description: The enablement status of secret scanning non-provider patterns

secret_scanning_delegated_alert_dismissa.description: The enablement status of secret scanning delegated alert dismissal

enforcement.description: The enforcement status for a security configuration

bodyParameters.description:
The enablement status of GitHub Advanced Security features. enabled will enable both Code Security and Secret Protection features.

Warning

code_security and secret_protection are deprecated values for this field. Prefer the individual code_security and secret_protection fields to set the status of these features.

configurations.descriptionHTML:
Creates a code security configuration in an enterprise.

The authenticated user must be an administrator of the enterprise in order to use this endpoint.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:enterprise scope to use this endpoint.

Note

Only installed security products may be specified in the request body. Specifying an uninstalled security product will result in a validation error.

Gets a code security configuration available in an enterprise.

The authenticated user must be an administrator of the enterprise in order to use this endpoint.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the read:enterprise scope to use this endpoint.

Note

Any security products that are not installed will report an unset status.

configurations.title: Delete a code security configuration for an enterprise

parameters.name: enterprise
parameters.description: The slug version of the enterprise name.

parameters.name: configuration_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the code security configuration.

configurations.descriptionHTML:
Deletes a code security configuration from an enterprise. Repositories attached to the configuration will retain their settings but will no longer be associated with the configuration.

The authenticated user must be an administrator for the enterprise to use this endpoint.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:enterprise scope to use this endpoint.

response.description: A header with no content is returned.

statusCodes.description: A header with no content is returned.

configurations.title: Attach an enterprise configuration to repositories

parameters.name: enterprise
parameters.description: The slug version of the enterprise name.

parameters.name: configuration_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the code security configuration.

bodyParameters.name: scope
bodyParameters.description: The type of repositories to attach the configuration to.

configurations.descriptionHTML:
Attaches an enterprise code security configuration to repositories. If the repositories specified are already attached to a configuration, they will be re-attached to the provided configuration.

If insufficient GHAS licenses are available to attach the configuration to a repository, only free features will be enabled.

The authenticated user must be an administrator for the enterprise to use this endpoint.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:enterprise scope to use this endpoint.

request.description: Example for attaching a configuration to some repositories

configurations.title: Set a code security configuration as a default for an enterprise

parameters.name: enterprise
parameters.description: The slug version of the enterprise name.

parameters.name: configuration_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the code security configuration.

bodyParameters.name: default_for_new_repos
bodyParameters.description: Specify which types of repository this security configuration should be applied to by default.

configurations.descriptionHTML:
Sets a code security configuration as a default to be applied to new repositories in your enterprise.

This configuration will be applied by default to the matching repository type when created, but only for organizations within the enterprise that do not already have a default code security configuration set.

The authenticated user must be an administrator for the enterprise to use this endpoint.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:enterprise scope to use this endpoint.

request.description: Set this configuration to be enabled by default on all new repositories.

value.name: octo-org recommended settings
value.description: This is a code security configuration for octo-org

configurations.title: Get repositories associated with an enterprise code security configuration

parameters.name: enterprise
parameters.description: The slug version of the enterprise name.

parameters.name: configuration_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the code security configuration.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: A cursor, as given in the Link header. If specified, the query only searches for results before this cursor. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: A cursor, as given in the Link header. If specified, the query only searches for results after this cursor. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: status
parameters.description:
A comma-separated list of statuses. If specified, only repositories with these attachment statuses will be returned.

Can be: all, attached, attaching, removed, enforced, failed, updating, removed_by_enterprise

configurations.descriptionHTML:
Lists the repositories associated with an enterprise code security configuration in an organization.

The authenticated user must be an administrator of the enterprise in order to use this endpoint.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the read:enterprise scope to use this endpoint.

items.description: Repositories associated with a code security configuration and attachment status

status.description: The attachment status of the code security configuration on the repository.

id.description: A unique identifier of the repository.

node_id.description: The GraphQL identifier of the repository.

full_name.description: The full, globally unique, name of the repository.

html_url.description: The URL to view the repository on GitHub.com.

fork.description: Whether the repository is a fork.

url.description: The URL to get more information about the repository from the GitHub API.

archive_url.description: A template for the API URL to download the repository as an archive.

assignees_url.description: A template for the API URL to list the available assignees for issues in the repository.

blobs_url.description: A template for the API URL to create or retrieve a raw Git blob in the repository.

branches_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about branches in the repository.

collaborators_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about collaborators of the repository.

comments_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about comments on the repository.

commits_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about commits on the repository.

compare_url.description: A template for the API URL to compare two commits or refs.

contents_url.description: A template for the API URL to get the contents of the repository.

contributors_url.description: A template for the API URL to list the contributors to the repository.

deployments_url.description: The API URL to list the deployments of the repository.

downloads_url.description: The API URL to list the downloads on the repository.

events_url.description: The API URL to list the events of the repository.

forks_url.description: The API URL to list the forks of the repository.

git_commits_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about Git commits of the repository.

git_refs_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about Git refs of the repository.

git_tags_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about Git tags of the repository.

issue_comment_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about issue comments on the repository.

issue_events_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about issue events on the repository.

issues_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about issues on the repository.

keys_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about deploy keys on the repository.

labels_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about labels of the repository.

languages_url.description: The API URL to get information about the languages of the repository.

merges_url.description: The API URL to merge branches in the repository.

milestones_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about milestones of the repository.

notifications_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about notifications on the repository.

pulls_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about pull requests on the repository.

releases_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about releases on the repository.

stargazers_url.description: The API URL to list the stargazers on the repository.

statuses_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about statuses of a commit.

subscribers_url.description: The API URL to list the subscribers on the repository.

subscription_url.description: The API URL to subscribe to notifications for this repository.

tags_url.description: The API URL to get information about tags on the repository.

teams_url.description: The API URL to list the teams on the repository.

trees_url.description: A template for the API URL to create or retrieve a raw Git tree of the repository.

hooks_url.description: The API URL to list the hooks on the repository.

configurations.title: Get code security configurations for an organization

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: target_type
parameters.description: The target type of the code security configuration

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: A cursor, as given in the Link header. If specified, the query only searches for results before this cursor. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: A cursor, as given in the Link header. If specified, the query only searches for results after this cursor. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

configurations.descriptionHTML:
Lists all code security configurations available in an organization.

The authenticated user must be an administrator or security manager for the organization to use this endpoint.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the read:org scope to use this endpoint.

Note

The enablement status will only be returned for installed security products.

example.name: GitHub recommended
example.description: Suggested settings for Dependabot, secret scanning, and code scanning.

example.name: High risk settings
example.description: This is a code security configuration for octo-org high risk repositories

id.description: The ID of the code security configuration

name.description: The name of the code security configuration. Must be unique within the organization.

configurations.descriptionHTML:
Creates a code security configuration in an organization.

The authenticated user must be an administrator or security manager for the organization to use this endpoint.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the write:org scope to use this endpoint.

Note

Only installed security products may be specified in the request body. Specifying an uninstalled security product will result in a validation error.

The authenticated user must be an administrator or security manager for the organization to use this endpoint.

configurations.title: Delete a code security configuration

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: configuration_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the code security configuration.

configurations.descriptionHTML:
Deletes the desired code security configuration from an organization. Repositories attached to the configuration will retain their settings but will no longer be associated with the configuration.

The authenticated user must be an administrator or security manager for the organization to use this endpoint.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the write:org scope to use this endpoint.

response.description: A header with no content is returned.

statusCodes.description: A header with no content is returned.

configurations.title: Attach a configuration to repositories

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: configuration_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the code security configuration.

bodyParameters.name: scope
bodyParameters.description: The type of repositories to attach the configuration to. selected means the configuration will be attached to only the repositories specified by selected_repository_ids

bodyParameters.name: selected_repository_ids
bodyParameters.description: An array of repository IDs to attach the configuration to. You can only provide a list of repository ids when the scope is set to selected.

configurations.descriptionHTML:
Attach a code security configuration to a set of repositories. If the repositories specified are already attached to a configuration, they will be re-attached to the provided configuration.

If insufficient GHAS licenses are available to attach the configuration to a repository, only free features will be enabled.

The authenticated user must be an administrator or security manager for the organization to use this endpoint.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the write:org scope to use this endpoint.

request.description: Example for attaching a configuration to some repositories

configurations.title: Set a code security configuration as a default for an organization

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: configuration_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the code security configuration.

bodyParameters.name: default_for_new_repos
bodyParameters.description: Specify which types of repository this security configuration should be applied to by default.

configurations.descriptionHTML:
Sets a code security configuration as a default to be applied to new repositories in your organization.

This configuration will be applied to the matching repository type (all, none, public, private and internal) by default when they are created.

The authenticated user must be an administrator or security manager for the organization to use this endpoint.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the write:org scope to use this endpoint.

Note

The enablement status will only be returned for installed security products.

request.description: Set this configuration to be enabled by default on all new repositories.

value.name: octo-org recommended settings
value.description: This is a code security configuration for octo-org

default_for_new_repos.description: Specifies which types of repository this security configuration is applied to by default.

configurations.title: Get repositories associated with a code security configuration

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: configuration_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the code security configuration.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: A cursor, as given in the Link header. If specified, the query only searches for results before this cursor. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: A cursor, as given in the Link header. If specified, the query only searches for results after this cursor. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: status
parameters.description:
A comma-separated list of statuses. If specified, only repositories with these attachment statuses will be returned.

Can be: all, attached, attaching, detached, removed, enforced, failed, updating, removed_by_enterprise

configurations.descriptionHTML:
Lists the repositories associated with a code security configuration in an organization.

The authenticated user must be an administrator or security manager for the organization to use this endpoint.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the read:org scope to use this endpoint.

items.description: Repositories associated with a code security configuration and attachment status

status.description: The attachment status of the code security configuration on the repository.

id.description: A unique identifier of the repository.

node_id.description: The GraphQL identifier of the repository.

full_name.description: The full, globally unique, name of the repository.

html_url.description: The URL to view the repository on GitHub.com.

fork.description: Whether the repository is a fork.

url.description: The URL to get more information about the repository from the GitHub API.

archive_url.description: A template for the API URL to download the repository as an archive.

assignees_url.description: A template for the API URL to list the available assignees for issues in the repository.

blobs_url.description: A template for the API URL to create or retrieve a raw Git blob in the repository.

branches_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about branches in the repository.

collaborators_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about collaborators of the repository.

comments_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about comments on the repository.

commits_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about commits on the repository.

compare_url.description: A template for the API URL to compare two commits or refs.

contents_url.description: A template for the API URL to get the contents of the repository.

contributors_url.description: A template for the API URL to list the contributors to the repository.

deployments_url.description: The API URL to list the deployments of the repository.

downloads_url.description: The API URL to list the downloads on the repository.

events_url.description: The API URL to list the events of the repository.

forks_url.description: The API URL to list the forks of the repository.

git_commits_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about Git commits of the repository.

git_refs_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about Git refs of the repository.

git_tags_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about Git tags of the repository.

issue_comment_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about issue comments on the repository.

issue_events_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about issue events on the repository.

issues_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about issues on the repository.

keys_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about deploy keys on the repository.

labels_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about labels of the repository.

languages_url.description: The API URL to get information about the languages of the repository.

merges_url.description: The API URL to merge branches in the repository.

milestones_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about milestones of the repository.

notifications_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about notifications on the repository.

pulls_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about pull requests on the repository.

releases_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about releases on the repository.

stargazers_url.description: The API URL to list the stargazers on the repository.

statuses_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about statuses of a commit.

subscribers_url.description: The API URL to list the subscribers on the repository.

subscription_url.description: The API URL to subscribe to notifications for this repository.

tags_url.description: The API URL to get information about tags on the repository.

teams_url.description: The API URL to list the teams on the repository.

trees_url.description: A template for the API URL to create or retrieve a raw Git tree of the repository.

hooks_url.description: The API URL to list the hooks on the repository.

configurations.title: Get the code security configuration associated with a repository

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

configurations.descriptionHTML:
Get the code security configuration that manages a repository's code security settings.

The authenticated user must be an administrator or security manager for the organization to use this endpoint.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo scope to use this endpoint.

configuration.name: octo-org recommended settings
configuration.description: This is a code security configuration for octo-org

schema.description: Code security configuration associated with a repository and attachment status

status.description: The attachment status of the code security configuration on the repository.

id.description: The ID of the code security configuration

name.description: The name of the code security configuration. Must be unique within the organization.

target_type.description: The type of the code security configuration.

description.description: A description of the code security configuration

advanced_security.description: The enablement status of GitHub Advanced Security

dependency_graph.description: The enablement status of Dependency Graph

dependabot_alerts.description: The enablement status of Dependabot alerts

dependabot_security_updates.description: The enablement status of Dependabot security updates

dependabot_delegated_alert_dismissal.description: The enablement status of Dependabot delegated alert dismissal

allow_advanced.description: Whether to allow repos which use advanced setup

code_scanning_default_setup.description: The enablement status of code scanning default setup

code_scanning_default_setup_options.description: Feature options for code scanning default setup

runner_type.description: Whether to use labeled runners or standard GitHub runners.

runner_label.description: The label of the runner to use for code scanning when runner_type is 'labeled'.

code_scanning_delegated_alert_dismissal.description: The enablement status of code scanning delegated alert dismissal

secret_scanning.description: The enablement status of secret scanning

secret_scanning_push_protection.description: The enablement status of secret scanning push protection

secret_scanning_delegated_bypass.description: The enablement status of secret scanning delegated bypass

secret_scanning_delegated_bypass_options.description: Feature options for secret scanning delegated bypass

reviewers.description: The bypass reviewers for secret scanning delegated bypass

reviewer_id.description: The ID of the team or role selected as a bypass reviewer

reviewer_type.description: The type of the bypass reviewer

security_configuration_id.description: The ID of the security configuration associated with this bypass reviewer

secret_scanning_validity_checks.description: The enablement status of secret scanning validity checks

secret_scanning_non_provider_patterns.description: The enablement status of secret scanning non-provider patterns

secret_scanning_delegated_alert_dismissa.description: The enablement status of secret scanning delegated alert dismissal

enforcement.description: The enforcement status for a security configuration

statusCodes.description: A header with no content is returned.
